items.quote: Only 2 buyer-side contact role(s) on this opp, below the multi-thread bar.
No CFO, SVP Finance, or CPO has joined any call or been named by the buyer as sponsoring this evaluation.
No dollar figure or ROI model presented in any call or Next Step entry.
No MSA/SOW/legal discussion in any read source.
Committed after the 22 May 2026 call; not confirmed completed in any later touchpoint.
items.source: Avoma call action items, 22 May 2026; Next_Step__c, 26 May 2026 entry

metrics.narrative: Manscaped has clearly named the business problems Zycus would solve: Becca West said Coupa's UI is 'not super user friendly' and that 'the PO isn't tied to the invoice... you have to go to multiple different screens,' and called the NetSuite connection 'clunky at best' (call, 1 Apr 2026). On 12 May 2026 the buyer described unmanaged tail spend causing surprise invoices with no approval trail. However, no dollar figure, cycle-time saving, or headcount impact has ever been quantified in any of the three read calls or the Next Step trail; Zycus offered to 'get competitive with pricing' (Next_Step__c, 2 Jun 2026) but never anchored that to a savings model. Heading into the 16 Jul 2026 RFP response (~1 week away), this is a live, material gap.

champion.narrative: Anita Newsom, Senior Director of Procurement, is a credible, self-declared champion: she personally 'recommended and onboarded Zycus at Ventura Foods and a construction company' and initiated this opportunity via a ProcureCon contact (1 Apr 2026 call). She appeared again on the 22 May 2026 demo. However, she has not been directly quoted or logged as active in the read evidence since that 22 May 2026 call (~48 days ago as of this sweep), with no visible push for economic-buyer access or urgency around the new 29 Jun 2026 RFP. Champion strength is real but currently developing/at-risk from recent passivity, not confirmed-and-active.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the sole named incumbent and alternative: Manscaped is 'on Coupa' for AP and procurement with a renewal approaching, which triggered this evaluation (Becca West, 1 Apr 2026). The buyer's complaints about Coupa (UI, PO-invoice disconnect, NetSuite integration) give Zycus a clear differentiation angle, but 'do nothing / renew Coupa as-is' remains a fully live fallback since no quantified case has been built to justify switching, and Coupa was never described as ruled out.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-articulated and consistent across calls: Becca West described Coupa's unfriendly UI and PO-invoice disconnect requiring multiple screens, plus a 'clunky at best' NetSuite integration (1 Apr 2026). On 12 May 2026 the buyer detailed a fully manual, email-driven procurement process with no centralized hub, specifically calling out unmanaged tail spend: 'we get an invoice... we don't know who that is, where it's come from.' This maps directly to the modules Zycus proposed (iSource, supplier management, tail-spend workflows via the Anna agent) demonstrated 22 May 2026.

sources: Absence across all three read Avoma calls and Next_Step__c / Next_Step_History__c
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review, MSA/SOW discussion, or PO requirement has surfaced in any read call, task, or Next Step entry. Expected at Shortlisted stage, but it means the 31 Aug 2026 close date (~53 days away) — only ~15 days after the 16 Aug 2026 decision date — has no supporting paper-process plan behind it.

economic_buyer.narrative: No CFO, SVP Finance, or CPO has joined any of the three read calls (1 Apr, 12 May, 22 May 2026) or been named by the buyer as sponsoring this evaluation. The most senior buyer voices on record are Anita Newsom (Senior Director of Procurement) and Becca West (Director of Financial and Accounting Systems) — influential, but below budget-approval authority for a $130,000 purchase. The account carries a Senior Vice President of Finance & Accounting at the account level, but nothing in the read evidence shows that person aware of or involved in this opportunity. With the buyer's internal decision due 16 Aug 2026 (~38 days away), this is the single largest structural risk on the deal.

decision_process.narrative: The process has run through two RFP cycles: an initial RFP received 10 Apr 2026 (responses due 17 Apr) that split into separate AP and procurement tracks (28 Apr 2026: 'they will move to include Zycus in RFP for procurement. We will not be included in RFP for AP'), followed by a fresh Procurement-only RFP issued 29 Jun 2026 (~10 days ago), responses due 16 Jul 2026, decision due 16 Aug 2026. Different buyer-side people own different parts of the process (Lisa Miller on AP, the procurement lead and Anita Newsom on procurement), but no confirmed final approver or sign-off chain beyond the RFP mechanics has been described on any call.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has been specific about evaluation criteria across two calls: Becca West wants a 'user-friendly interface, integrated PO-invoice workflow, and analytics on RFP responses' (12 May 2026), and flagged NetSuite and LinkSquares integration as critical. On 22 May 2026, buyer attendees probed integration depth (Teams, LinkSquares), user/role permissioning, and the source of third-party supplier-risk data (D&B, EcoVadis), all answered on the call. Criteria are now scoped to procurement functions only (sourcing, supplier management, tail-spend control) following the 29 Jun 2026 scope narrowing.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Manscaped kept Zycus alive through a second, narrower RFP cycle on 29 Jun 2026, and product reception on calls has been consistently positive, but the deal still lacks an engaged economic buyer, a quantified business case, and a credible paper-process plan for its 31 Aug 2026 close date.

deal_risk.text: No economic buyer has been identified or engaged at any point in the read evidence, a MEDDPICC gap that structurally caps confidence in this deal regardless of stage.
The 31 Aug 2026 close date leaves only ~15 days after the 16 Aug 2026 decision date for contracting, legal, and signature, an unrealistic window with no mutual close plan on record.
The buyer's do-nothing alternative (renewing Coupa) remains fully viable since no quantified ROI case has been made to justify switching costs.

Only 2 buyer-side contact roles are logged on the opportunity, well below the multi-thread bar, leaving the deal effectively single-threaded through the procurement lead and Lisa Miller for RFP mechanics.

win_position.text: Manscaped included Zycus in a new Procurement-only RFP issued 29 Jun 2026 (~10 days ago), response due 16 Jul and decision due 16 Aug 2026, keeping Zycus alive for a second consecutive RFP cycle (Next_Step__c).

No economic buyer (CFO, SVP Finance, or CPO) has joined a call or been named as sponsoring this evaluation across all three read calls (1 Apr, 12 May, 22 May 2026); the procurement lead running the RFP joined Manscaped only about three months ago (12 May 2026 call) and cannot independently approve a $130,000 purchase.

No quantified ROI or savings case has been built despite repeated qualitative pain (Coupa UI, PO-to-invoice disconnect, NetSuite integration 'clunky at best' - Becca West, 1 Apr 2026; tail-spend leakage - 12 May 2026); Zycus offered to 'get competitive with pricing' (Next Step, 2 Jun 2026) but no business case exists to justify the spend over a routine Coupa renewal.

Champion Anita Newsom has personally implemented Zycus at two prior employers (Ventura Foods and a construction company) and initiated this introduction via a ProcureCon contact (1 Apr 2026 call), giving Zycus a credible internal advocate.

Scope was narrowed from full Procure-to-Pay to Procurement-only in the new RFP (29 Jun 2026), with AP evaluated on a separate track that already excluded Zycus (12 May 2026 call: 'we have not been included in RFP for AP'), reducing deal size and urgency.

deal_momentum.text: Since the last read demo on 22 May 2026 (~48 days ago), the only confirmed two-way buyer contact in the evidence read is an incoming email reply on 16 Jun 2026 (~23 days ago); the most recent Next Step update (29 Jun 2026, ~10 days ago) logs the new RFP but is not corroborated by a fresh direct buyer exchange in the calls read.
The 29 Jun 2026 RFP reissuance, with a hard response date of 16 Jul 2026 (~1 week away) and decision date of 16 Aug 2026 (~5 weeks away), gives the deal concrete, dated forward milestones.

The eProcurement/Insights Studio follow-up demo committed by Zycus after the 22 May 2026 call has not been logged as completed in any of the three read touchpoints, a Zycus-owed deliverable now roughly seven weeks outstanding.

Pricing for the reduced Procurement-only scope was shared 9 Jun 2026 with a promised buyer response by 18 Jun 2026; no confirmed reply to that pricing is recorded before the 29 Jun 2026 scope-change RFP.

customer_commitment.text: Manscaped voluntarily re-included Zycus in a fresh RFP cycle on 29 Jun 2026 rather than defaulting to the Coupa renewal, signaling continued (if narrowed) interest.
On the 22 May 2026 demo, buyer attendees gave unprompted positive feedback ('Teams integration is great,' supplier risk visibility called 'super cool') with no stated gaps.

Champion Anita Newsom's unsolicited endorsement, based on two prior successful Zycus deployments (1 Apr 2026 call), is a durable proof point, though her direct engagement has thinned since 22 May 2026.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are tagged on the opportunity, but the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty. The Merlin attachment is Zycus-driven, not a reflection of documented buyer commentary.
• The April 1 call (26 minutes) with Lisa Miller, Becca West and Anita Newsom produced action items strictly on Showpad creation, supplier-management module questions covering quality and spec docs, and demo recordings spanning intake-to-pay, supplier performance management, project management and sourcing. Merlin, agentic AI and autonomous negotiation were not raised by the buyer.
• The May 12 follow-up was a short 18-minute session and also produced no AI signal. The buyer's focus is on confirming module functionality, especially spec document storage, and consuming information via Showpad. With no buyer AI quotes and the commentary fields blank, this lands at Curious with low confidence.
